2606.26880.title: Heterogeneous Neural Predictivity from Language Models During Naturalistic Comprehension
2606.26880.paper_summary: Language-model representations provide structured, high-dimensional annotations of naturalistic language stimuli and can serve as informative neural predictors during comprehension. We analyzed locked derived data from Brain Treebank, MEG-MASC, and Podcast ECoG with eight frozen language models, blocked encoding models, and matched temporal, nuisance, and representation-capacity controls. Positive held-out prediction and gains over low-level baselines were widespread in source-level summaries. Across Brain Treebank and Podcast ECoG, 67 of 432 evaluable rows met a controlled predictive-only criterion, and model-side feature ablations changed prediction scores in most evaluable source rows. Brain-derived, timing-linked, acoustic, and implanted-signal controls confirmed component-level sensitivity of the analysis pipeline. These findings show that language-model-derived quantities can annotate neural activity during natural speech and text comprehension. Participant-level matched-control advantages were localized rather than uniform, response-profile and feature-specificity contrasts bounded representational or computational interpretations, and complete co-indexed integrated interpretation will require future jointly indexed coverage. Together, the analyses identify language-model features as useful neural predictors and separate predictive usefulness from claims about shared neural organization or language-processing computations.
2606.26880.paper_summary_zh: 語言模型表示提供了結構化的高維度自然語言刺激註解，並且可以在理解過程中作為有用的神經預測因子。我們分析了來自Brain Treebank、MEG-MASC和Podcast ECoG的鎖定衍生數據，使用了八個凍結的語言模型、區塊編碼模型，以及匹配的時間、干擾和表徵能力控制。正向的保留預測和相較於低級基準的增益在源級摘要中廣泛存在。在Brain Treebank和Podcast ECoG中，432個可評估行中有67個滿足受控的僅預測標準，模型側的特徵消融在大多數可評估的源行中改變了預測分數。來自大腦的、與時間相關的、聲學的和植入信號的控制確認了分析管道的組件級敏感性。這些發現表明，語言模型衍生的量可以在自然語音和文本理解過程中註解神經活動。參與者級的匹配控制優勢是局部的而非均勻的，反應輪廓和特徵特異性對比限制了表徵或計算解釋，且完整的共同索引綜合解釋將需要未來共同索引的覆蓋。總體而言，這些分析確定了語言模型特徵作為有用的神經預測因子，並將預測的有用性與關於共享神經組織或語言處理計算的主張分開。
2606.26880.author: Xiao Jia et.al.
